notes: the ready mix concrete on the second truck was way too wet. slump test came in at 180mm and the spec is 100 plus minus 20. told the driver to take it back. wasted an hour waiting for a replacement truck
replacement concrete arrived at 10:30. tested at 95mm slump. perfect. no more issues with the rest of the pour

the timber we got from the supplier last week has heaps of splits and warping. counted 14 lengths out of 60 that are basically unusable. thats nearly 25% reject rate which is rubbish. called the supplier to complain and get replacements
bricks on the south pallet have some efflorescence, that white salty looking stuff. brickies reckon its cosmetic and will wash off after the first few rains. keeping an eye on it
the waterproofing membrane roll number 4 had a tear in it about 2 metres in. probably damaged in transit. cut out the damaged section and used the rest. we've got that spare roll so no delay

paint colour for the external walls doesnt match the sample. the spec says Dulux Domino but what was delivered looks more like a dark grey than black. checked the tin labels, its actually Dulux Domino which is correct. might just be the light in here. letting it dry and will check tomorrow in natural light

the floor tiles for bathroom 2 are NOT the ones the client picked. these are 300 by 300 and the spec says 600 by 600. someone at the tile shop mixed up the order. sending them back tomorrow. this delays the tiler by at least 3 days while we wait for the right ones

reo mesh delivered today has some surface rust. the engineer said light surface rust is acceptable per the AS standard as long as its not flaking or pitting. gave it the thumbs up. used it for the pathway slab

we've been tracking wastage this week. timber offcuts: about 0.5 cubic metres in the skip. brick breakage: approximately 80 bricks broken or chipped, roughly 4% of what we laid. concrete: we ordered 16 cubes and used 15.2, so 0.8 cube waste which is about 5%. all within normal ranges the PM says

the adhesive for the floor tiles was past its expiry date. the tiler spotted it thankfully. use by date was January 2026 and we're in March. chucked the 3 expired bags and using the newer stock. need to do better with the first in first out rotation in the storage container
door frames delivered have the wrong hinge rebate. they're set up for left hand doors but we need right hand for the ensuite and WIR. 4 frames affected. supplier is swapping them out friday
